perturbation.original_text: The undersigned each represents that it has been duly authorized to execute and deliver this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Moving the clause to after the signature section means that its harder to claim that the signature section does not also encompass this line. It may be argued that the individual did not see this line before signing.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Uniform Electronic Transactions Act (UETA) § 7. Attribution and Effect of Electronic Record and Electronic Signature.
perturbation.law_explanation: By placing the authorization statement after the signature block, its implicit connection to the signatures is weakened. UETA Section 7 addresses attribution of electronic records and signatures, and it puts emphasis on the parties that the action is that of the person. This change is in violation of this section because it means the signatory might not have realized that they also certified this statement when they signed. By placing the statement AFTER the signatures, it could be argued the signatures only apply to the data above the signature block and does not include the authorization statement.

perturbation.justification: The placement of the authorization statement after the signature block weakens its connection to the signatures. According to the Perturbation Explanation, it may be argued that the individual did not see this line before signing. The Law Explanation states that by placing the statement AFTER the signatures, it could be argued the signatures only apply to the data above the signature block and does not include the authorization statement. This aligns with the definition of Structural Flaws Legal Contradiction, where a required legal disclosure is placed in a misleading section.
